title: Currant Jelly Stools - Intussusception

patient.name: Child Tariq (9 months)
patient.persona: colicky abdominal pain, drawing up legs

chief_complaint: 9-month-old with sudden episodes of severe colicky abdominal pain, drawing legs up, occurring every 15-20 minutes. Between episodes appears normal. Now passed currant jelly stool (blood and mucus).

physical_exam.abdomen: Sausage-shaped mass in right upper quadrant, emptiness in right lower quadrant (Dance sign), mild distension

hidden_data.us_abd: Target sign (pseudokidney sign) in right upper quadrant, ileocolic intussusception, no free fluid, good vascular flow

key_learning_points: Triad: colicky pain + currant jelly stools + abdominal mass
Surgical reduction if enema fails or peritonitis

patient_responses.pain: He screams and pulls his legs up, then seems fine for a while
patient_responses.stool: He just passed something that looks like red jelly
patient_responses.feeding: He vomited after the last feed

feedback.incorrect_message: This is intussusception. The currant jelly stool + ultrasound target sign confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Intussusception: Air enema under fluoroscopic guidance is both diagnostic and therapeutic. Success rate >80%. Surgical reduction if enema fails or peritonitis. NPO, IV fluids, NG tube. Time is critical - risk of bowel ischemia increases with delay.
